title: armeria maritima subsp. halleri
intro: Armeria maritima subsp. halleri, also known as the sea thrift or alpine sea pink, is a subspecies of the flowering plant Armeria maritima. This perennial plant is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in reducing inflammation and soothing digestive issues. The plant's properties are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, providing health benefits such as improved cardiovascular health and immune system function. Armeria maritima subsp. halleri can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive problems and respiratory issues.
